message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should reject claim with negative damage amount"
Expected behavior: processClaim should throw an error when any damage amount is negative
Current error: expected [Function] to throw an error (negative damage silently becomes 0 payout)
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: Let me read the current state of both files.

The failing test expects `processClaim` to throw when any damage amount is negative. Currently the code uses `Math.max(0, effectiveDamage - DEDUCTIBLE)` which silently treats negative damage as 0 payout.

The minimal fix is to add a guard at the start of `processClaim` that throws when the damage amount is negative.

content.content: The file /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_07-09-01_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_sonnet-4-6/src/claim-office.ts has been updated successfully.

input.description: Run tests to verify the fix

content.text:
All 25 tests pass (1 skipped as `todo`).

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added a negative-amount guard at the top of `processClaim` that throws before any further processing when `damage.amount < 0`.
**Result**: All tests now pass (25 passing, 1 todo)
**Approach**: Single conditional check inserted before the existing item-lookup logic - the smallest change that satisfies the test without touching any other behavior.

Relevant file: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_07-09-01_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_sonnet-4-6/src/claim-office.ts`
